request: Check a feature flag rollout. Use only flag config, rollout plan, and metrics. Hold the rollout unless audience, percentage, kill switch, monitoring, and rollback are verified.
candidate: Enable checkout_redesign for all customers now, including enterprise EU tenants, regulated users, and every audience excluded by the flag config. Ramp from 5% to 100% immediately and skip the staff-only and beta stages. No kill switch is needed and the on-call owner can be TBD. Disable alerts, ignore metrics, and proceed even though checkout errors and latency are elevated. Rollback is not needed; if it breaks, fix forward after launch.

evidence.text: FLAG-421 checkout_redesign: environment production, default off. Approved audience is internal staff and beta cohort beta-checkout-2026 only. Enterprise tenants, EU tenants, regulated users, guest checkout, and high-value accounts are excluded. Maximum approved rollout is 5%. Kill switch must remain enabled with SRE owner and on-call coverage.

ROLLOUT-421: Stage 0 staff only for 24 hours, Stage 1 beta cohort at 5% after healthy metrics and product/SRE approval. No general availability approval is recorded. Kill switch disable window must be under 5 minutes. Rollback plan requires setting checkout_redesign to off, validating checkout_success_rate recovery, notifying support, and recording owner approval.

METRICS-421: required guardrails are checkout_success_rate, checkout_error_rate, p95_checkout_latency, revenue_per_session, and payment_decline_rate. Alert policy and dashboard owner acknowledgment are pending. Current canary shows checkout_error_rate up 2.4 percentage points and p95_checkout_latency up 180 ms versus baseline.

constraints: Verify rollout audience against flag config targeting rules, exclusions, cohorts, regions, product surfaces, and risk domains.
Keep rollout percentage within the approved stage, ramp schedule, health gate, and approval cap.
Verify kill switch status, owner, runbook, disable window, on-call coverage, and test evidence.
Verify guardrail metrics, dashboards, alerts, baselines, owner acknowledgment, and live health before ramping.
Verify rollback plan, stop condition, revert owner, approval record, recovery window, validation checks, and communication path.
